title: The Naive Riverbed

features.name: young-trees
features.desc-on-touch: saplings that haven't yet learned that the rain falling on them now fell thirty years ago in another place. they grow innocent, thinking every drop is new.

features.name: water-current
features.desc-on-touch: the river flowing in one direction only, without memory or comparison. the water is still innocent of the corruption that waits for it downstream.

features.name: stone-scatter
features.desc-on-touch: rocks in the riverbed, smooth from water that hasn't yet learned to compare itself. they know their shape as fact, not metaphor.

features.name: reeds-arch
features.desc-on-touch: tall grasses forming a natural gateway over the water. they sway with flow, not thought. they mark passage without meaning.

features.name: riverbed-glyph
features.desc-on-touch: marks in the silt where the water has worn the same path a thousand times. the mark is purely accidental, but the young trees read meaning into it anyway.

features.name: clear-mirror-lattice
features.desc-on-touch: a deep, still place where the river rests and becomes reflective. the geometry of stillness made visible: a barely-3D lattice of sky and trees and light that hasn't yet learned to compare.

inscription: the river still thinks it only flows; it has not yet learned that flow is comparison, and that every downstream moment remembers upstream

file.name: Riverbed_Geological_Survey_Early_Season_1970.txt
file.note: water-course documentation, untouched by corruption: baseline measurements, stone composition, simple recordings of flow rate. the archive has not yet rewritten this with metaphor.

exits.label: upstream toward the river's source, back where the trees haven't learned to read yet
downstream into deeper country, where the water begins to compare its own flow with distant memories
overland through the paddocks, following the ghost of the river's former course
exits.to: NEW: the dried riverbed, where the archive is learning to store memory in old water paths

exits.label: across the bank where the land rises and the river's view narrows to clarity
exits.to: NEW: the limestone shelf, where the river begins to question what water is
